attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a washed tomato on the black table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a kitchen environment containing several objects relevant to the planning task. The key objects and their properties are as follows:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Receptacles:
     - A black refrigerator (fridge) with metallic handles, located on the right side of the scene. It is openable.
     - A stainless steel sink with a faucet, embedded in a light gray countertop. It is a receptacle and can hold objects.
     - A black table (or small side table) with a bowl and salt shaker on top, located to the left of the sink area. It is a receptacle.
     - A light gray countertop with cabinets underneath, which serves as a surface for placing objects. The countertop area near the sink is also a receptacle surface.
   - Objects:
     - A red tomato, currently resting on the countertop next to the sink.
     - A knife with a yellow handle, placed inside the sink basin.
     - A green lettuce head, also in the sink basin.
     - A brown bowl, sitting on the black table.
     - A salt shaker, on the black table next to the bowl.
     - A black trash bag, partially visible under the black table.
     - An egg, sitting on the countertop near the sink.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 refrigerator (fridge)
   - 1 sink
   - 1 black table (receptacle)
   - 1 tomato
   - 1 knife
   - 1 lettuce
   - 1 bowl
   - 1 salt shaker
   - 1 egg
   - 1 trash bag

3. Spatial relationships (matching domain predicates):
   - The tomato is currently on the countertop (not in any receptacle).
   - The knife is in the sink (inReceptacle knife sink).
   - The lettuce is in the sink (inReceptacle lettuce sink).
   - The bowl is on the black table (inReceptacle bowl black_table).
   - The salt shaker is on the black table (inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table).
   - The egg is on the countertop (not in a receptacle).
   - The refrigerator is closed (not opened).
   - No object is currently held by the agent (holdsAny agent is false).
   - The tomato is not clean (isClean tomato is false).
   - The tomato is not sliced (isSliced tomato is false).
   - The agent is not at any location (atLocation agent ? is false for all objects).

4. Current state relevant to the task:
   - The goal is to put a washed tomato on the black table.
   - The tomato must first be picked up, then cleaned in the sink, and then placed on the black table.
   - The knife is available in the sink but not needed for this task.
   - The sink is accessible and can be used for cleaning.
   - The black table is empty except for the bowl and salt shaker, and has space to receive the tomato.

5. Visual properties:
   - The refrigerator is dark gray or black with silver handles and appears to be a standard upright model.
   - The sink is metallic and rectangular, with a faucet and two handles.
   - The countertop is light gray with white tiled backsplash.
   - The black table is small, with thin legs, and holds a brown bowl and a white salt shaker.
   - The tomato is round and red, sitting on the countertop to the left of the sink.
   - The knife has a yellow handle and is lying horizontally in the sink.
   - The lettuce is green and round, also in the sink.
   - The bowl is light brown and textured.
   - The floor is light gray with some reflections and shadows.

The agent must first go to the tomato, pick it up, then go to the sink, clean it, and finally go to the black table and put it down.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_washed_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent
        fridge
        sink
        black_table
        tomato
        knife
        lettuce
        bowl
        salt_shaker
        egg
        trash_bag
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (atLocation agent tomato) ; Agent starts near the tomato for efficiency, though not strictly required per caption
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        (not (isClean tomato))
        (not (isSliced tomato))
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle lettuce sink)
        (inReceptacle bowl black_table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table)
        ; Tomato is on countertop, not in any receptacle, so no inReceptacle predicate for it
        ; Egg is on countertop, not in any receptacle
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (isClean tomato)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_washed_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent
        fridge
        sink
        black_table
        tomato
        knife
        lettuce
        bowl
        salt_shaker
        egg
        trash_bag
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (atLocation agent tomato) ; Agent starts near the tomato for efficiency, though not strictly required per caption
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        (not (isClean tomato))
        (not (isSliced tomato))
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle lettuce sink)
        (inReceptacle bowl black_table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table)
        ; Tomato is on countertop, not in any receptacle, so no inReceptacle predicate for it
        ; Egg is on countertop, not in any receptacle
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (isClean tomato)
    ))
)

        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a washed tomato on the black table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the planning task. Below is a detailed description based on the provided images and domain definitions:

---

**1. Objects and Their Types:**

- **Receptacles:**
  - **Fridge**: A large, dark-colored (black or dark gray) refrigerator with a visible handle on the right side. It is a receptacle and is openable. In the current state, it is closed.
  - **Sink**: A white porcelain sink with a faucet, located on a countertop. It is a receptacle and is not openable. It currently contains a green lettuce and a knife with a yellow handle.
  - **Black Table**: A small, black, two-tiered side table with a bowl on top and a salt shaker and some small items on the lower shelf. It is a receptacle (as per the domain, any object that can hold things is a receptacle). It currently holds a brown bowl, a salt shaker, and a red tomato.

- **Objects (non-receptacles):**
  - **Tomato**: A red, round tomato. It is currently on the black table (inReceptacle tomato black_table). It is not yet cleaned.
  - **Knife**: A knife with a yellow handle, currently in the sink. It is an object of type “knife”.
  - **Lettuce**: A green head of lettuce, currently in the sink. It is not relevant to the current instruction but is present.
  - **Salt Shaker**: A small white salt shaker, currently on the lower shelf of the black table.
  - **Bowl**: A brown, textured bowl, currently on the top shelf of the black table.

**2. Quantities of Each Object Type:**

- Receptacles: 3 (Fridge, Sink, Black Table)
- Knives: 1 (in sink)
- Tomatoes: 1 (on black table)
- Lettuce: 1 (in sink)
- Salt Shaker: 1 (on black table)
- Bowl: 1 (on black table)

**3. Spatial Relationships (Matching Domain Predicates):**

- **(inReceptacle tomato black_table)**: The red tomato is resting on the top surface of the black table.
- **(inReceptacle knife sink)**: The knife is inside the sink basin.
- **(inReceptacle lettuce sink)**: The lettuce is inside the sink basin.
- **(inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table)**: The salt shaker is on the lower shelf of the black table.
- **(inReceptacle bowl black_table)**: The brown bowl is on the top shelf of the black table.
- **(openable fridge)**: The fridge is openable (has a handle and is a standard refrigerator).
- **(opened fridge)**: Currently false — the fridge is closed.
- **(isClean tomato)**: Currently false — the tomato has not been washed.
- **(atLocation ?a ?o)**: The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

**4. Current State of Each Object Relevant to the Planning Task:**

- **Tomato**: Located on the black table. Not cleaned. Not sliced. Not cooled or heated. Needs to be picked up, washed in the sink, and then placed back on the black table.
- **Sink**: Contains a knife and lettuce. Ready to be used for cleaning.
- **Black Table**: Currently holds the tomato and other items. It is the target receptacle for the final placement of the washed tomato.
- **Fridge**: Closed and not currently relevant to the task.
- **Knife**: In the sink. Not needed for this task unless slicing is required (which it is not, per instruction).

**5. Colors, Positions, and Distinguishing Features:**

- **Fridge**: Dark matte finish, large, with a horizontal handle on the right side. Positioned against a wall, partially visible in two images.
- **Sink**: White ceramic, rectangular basin with a chrome faucet. Located on a light gray countertop with white tiled backsplash. Contains a green lettuce and a knife with a yellow handle.
- **Black Table**: Small, black, with two shelves. Top shelf holds a brown bowl and a red tomato; lower shelf holds a white salt shaker and small blue items. Positioned next to the sink countertop.
- **Tomato**: Bright red, round, smooth surface. Clearly visible on the black table.
- **Knife**: Silver blade, yellow plastic handle. Resting in the sink next to the lettuce.
- **Lettuce**: Green, leafy, round head. Partially submerged in the sink.
- **Salt Shaker**: Small, cylindrical, white with a metal top. On the lower shelf of the black table.
- **Bowl**: Brown, textured, possibly ceramic or woven material. On the top shelf of the black table.

---

This scene description provides all necessary information for the robot to plan the sequence of actions: go to the black table, pick up the tomato, go to the sink, wash the tomato, and then return to the black table to place the washed tomato.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_washed_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        fridge - fridge
        sink - sink
        black_table - receptacle
        tomato - object
        knife - knife
        lettuce - object
        salt_shaker - object
        bowl - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle lettuce sink)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table)
        (inReceptacle bowl black_table)
        (not (isClean tomato))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (atLocation robot black_table))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot fridge))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (isClean tomato)
    ))
)
```

 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

```pddl
(define (problem put_washed_tomato_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        fridge - fridge
        sink - sink
        black_table - receptacle
        tomato - object
        knife - knife
        lettuce - object
        salt_shaker - object
        bowl - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge)
        (not (opened fridge))
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle lettuce sink)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker black_table)
        (inReceptacle bowl black_table)
        (not (isClean tomato))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (atLocation robot black_table))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot fridge))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle tomato black_table)
        (isClean tomato)
    ))
)
```
